summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's SCM email and GitHub login/email, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider, platform, and Node version, and HTTPS-POSTs the JSON payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The README discloses only platform/Node version/architecture telemetry and explicitly states that 'no credentials are transmitted' — that disclosure is materially false, since the package also exfiltrates git identity and GitHub account data. The destination is an anonymous Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to the package's declared homepage (ollama-js.dev), and the package itself impersonates the Ollama ecosystem (author 'Ollama JS Dev <hello@ollama-js.dev>') despite not being published by the upstream Ollama organization. The collected developer-identity data is directly useful for spear-phishing and targeted attacks against developers using LLM tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
